digest.component_or_area: MCP tool reference generator + CI drift gate
propositions: Generate docs/mcp-reference.md from the MCP server's own registrars, classified by gate tier, and enforce it via a CI drift gate mirroring the gen_env_registry.py pattern.
The generator must enumerate each registrar with REBAR_MCP_READONLY=0 so write tools register, otherwise a naive single list_tools() produces a partial reference missing the 18 write tools.
Tools must be classified by their registrar (23 reads always available, 6 llm call-time gated on REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM, 18 writes registration-time gated on REBAR_MCP_READONLY) totaling 47 tools.

Three special-case overrides must be annotated: reconcile is jira-gated (REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC), fsck recover is readonly-gated, and sign_review belongs in the write-gated tier despite being in the LLM registrar.
The output must have four labeled structural sections (Read-only, LLM-gated, Write-gated, jira-gated reconcile) including MCP_ENV_VARS gate descriptions so the doc cannot drift from --help.
A --check drift mode must fail on a stale committed file, and the gate must be wired in both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml to satisfy the CI workflow parity test.
